items.title: Reed Jobs would rather talk about curing cancer than his last name
items.summary: When we last sat down with Jobs at TechCrunch Disrupt nearly three years ago, his firm Yosemite was brand new and biotech was still reeling from its post-pandemic crash. Now, the venture outfit has a team of 17; a cluster of blockbuster drugs are all losing patent protection in roughly the same window, creating all kinds of new opportunities; and AI has gone from a curiosity to, in Jobs's words, a huge part of what Yosemite does. "I didn't expect Yosemite to be moving this fast," he said.

It’s time for another Razer collab, and this time it’s with Cinnamoroll, a Sanrio character to contrast the much more well-known Hello Kitty. On that note, this collaboration comes in the form of a special edition Kraken Kitty V2 BT headset, much like the Hello Kitty one from 2024. In the same vein, you can […]

items.title: SK Hynix's historic US stock market listing is a bet that the AI boom is breaking the memory chip industry's decades-long boom-and-bust cycle (Bloomberg)
items.summary:
Bloomberg:
SK Hynix's historic US stock market listing is a bet that the AI boom is breaking the memory chip industry's decades-long boom-and-bust cycle  —  South Korean memory chipmaker SK Hynix Inc. just pulled off the largest public listing by a foreign company in US market history.

items.title: An analysis of 1M+ social media posts from April 24 to June 30: ~25% of longform posts with 250+ words were fully AI-generated; on LinkedIn, the figure was 41% (Max Spero/Pangram Labs)
items.summary:
Max Spero / Pangram Labs:
An analysis of 1M+ social media posts from April 24 to June 30: ~25% of longform posts with 250+ words were fully AI-generated; on LinkedIn, the figure was 41%  —  A first look at analytics from our Pangram Chrome extension  —  Two months ago, we launched our Chrome extension to help combat the rising slop problem on social media.

items.title: US cybersecurity agency CISA had to build its incident playbook during the incident, agency reveals
items.summary: Independent cybersecurity journalist Brian Krebs reported in May that a security researcher with cyber firm GitGuardian alerted him to reams of exposed passwords stored in a publicly accessible GitHub repository, which an employee of a CISA contractor had uploaded.

items.title: Chinese Gamers Allowed By Courts To Inherit Accounts Of Dead Gamer Relatives
items.summary:
Chinese courts recently ruled in favour of gamers in the country, allowing for multiple Chinese families to “inherit the game accounts of deceased relatives”. The cases were part of a drawn-out legal issue in China, which addresses the subject of digital ownership, long after the owner of said game account has passed. The news was […]

The post Chinese Gamers Allowed By Courts To Inherit Accounts Of Dead Gamer Relatives appeared first on Lowyat.NET.

items.title: Phia accused of ‘cookie stuffing,’ taking affiliate credit on purchases it didn’t earn
items.summary: Phia, the shopping startup founded by Bill Gates’ daughter, Phoebe, and her friend Sophia Kianni is under fire for a practice known as “cookie stuffing,” which helped the product receive commissions and credit for sales it did not actually generate, per a Bloomberg investigation.

items.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
items.summary: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."

items.title: Pack the BougeRV 2026 telescopic camping light for your outdoor adventures while down at $75 low right now
items.summary:
Update: Amazon has dropped the price on this camping light back to the $75 low during a limited-time Lightning sale.

BougeRV 2026 Telescopic Camping Light

Buy for $81 (Reg. $100)

What we love

Got plenty of outdoor adventures planned for the rest of summer? Well, this handy BougeRV camping light is a versatile device that you should definitely consider adding to your kit, as its 15,600mAh battery allows for up to 102 hours of lighting on each full charge. Not only that, but it goes from a 2.4-pound compact and portable size and extends up to 5.5 feet tall, complete with adjustable lights and three modes (daylight, amber, red).

items.title: Sharge’s ICEMAG 3 10,000mAh Qi2.2 25W power bank with active cooling 3.0 is down at its second-lowest $68 price, more
items.summary: Through its official Amazon storefront, Sharge is offering the ICEMAG 3 10,000mAh Qi2.2 25W MagSafe Power Bank going for $67.89 shipped. It’s down from $80 here, which was the lowest price until last month’s Prime Day gave us a new $64 low. If you missed out then, you only have to shell out an extra $4 here today for the second-lowest price we have tracked in its history. If you don’t mind dropping down to 15W charging speeds, you can also save a bit more with the ICEMAG 2 model at $52.90 shipped, down from $70, or there are additional Sharge deals in the official Amazon storefront here.

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
items.summary: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAI's senior leadership, including a longtime former employee.

items.title: One new AI platform lets you use GPT, Gemini, Claude, and more at the same time, now $60 for life
items.summary: Getting the best answer out of AI usually means running the same prompt through ChatGPT, then Claude, then Gemini, and comparing them yourself. ChatPlayground AI does that comparison in one window, and you also get access to all the models you usually pay for monthly. Right now, a lifetime subscription is also on sale for $59.97 (reg. $619).

items.title: Malaysia EV registrations nearly double in June 2026, Proton e.MAS 5 leads again
items.summary: Malaysia’s electric vehicle (EV) market continue to show strong momentum in June 2026, with 6,215 units registered according to the latest JPJ data. That’s an increase of 89.9% compared to 3,272 EVs registered in June 2025. EVs accounted for 8.52% of Malaysia’s 72,943 total vehicle registrations in June, making it the second-highest monthly EV market […]

items.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
items.summary: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.

items.title: A new app, HyperTexting, turns the open web into a scrollable social media-like feed
items.summary: HyperTexting's new app aims to make the open web feel more like social media by turning websites, blogs, newsletters, and podcasts into a scrollable feed, while also making it easier to post to your own website.

items.title: Disney+ is considering a free streaming tier, report says

items.title: Netflix could be planning ‘always-on’ live TV channels

items.title: EU threatens Meta with fines over addictive features on Facebook and Instagram
items.summary: The tech giant is in breach of the Digital Services Act by focusing on features like infinite scroll, autoplay, push notifications, and the highly personalized recommendation algorithms, the European Commission said.

items.title: Florida ransomware negotiator convicted for helping ransomware gang extort US companies
items.summary: A third ransomware negotiator has been jailed for helping a notorious ransomware group extort American victim companies into paying the hackers.

items.title: Redditor Exposes Fake 16TB SSD Scam After Finding Weights And A microSD Card Inside
items.summary:
One Reddit user recently shared their discovery of a fake 16TB SSD purchased from eBay for under $30 (~RM122). Instead of a high-capacity drive, the enclosure only contained a small board, a microSD card, and some weights hot-glued inside to mimic the feel of a legitimate SSD. u/Hartkralle explained that they regularly purchase suspicious storage […]

The post Redditor Exposes Fake 16TB SSD Scam After Finding Weights And A microSD Card Inside appeared first on Lowyat.NET.
